gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/114)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/114)
text: قَالَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) رُبَّمَا أُتِيَ بِالْمَائِدَةِ فَأَرَادَ بَعْضُ الْقَوْمِ أَنْ يَغْسِلَ يَدَهُ فَيَقُولُ مَنْ كَانَتْ يَدُهُ نَظِيفَةً فَلَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَغْسِلَ يَدَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad, from his father, from Suleyman Al Ja’fary who said,

‘Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> said, when sometimes they come with the meal, so one of the people intended to wash his hands, so he<sup>asws</sup> was saying: ‘The one whose hands are clean, so there is no problem if he were to eat without washing his hands’.
en.sarwar: 13. Ahmad has narrated from his father from Sulayman al-Ja‘fariy who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, when food was made available and certain people had not washed their hands, would say, ‘Those whose hands are clean can eat without washing their hands.’”
